column1: Your list is generally accurate, but there are some corrections and additions to be made based on the search results and Buddhist texts:
King Bandhuma and Queen Bandhumatī - His parents14
Khanda and Tissa - His two male Chief Disciples14
Candā and Candamittā - His two female Chief Disciples1
Punabbasumitta and Nāga - His noble male lay supporters1
Sirīmā and Uttarā - His female lay supporters1
Abhibhū and Sambhava - His chief disciples45
Soṇa and Uttara - His chief disciples4
Vidhura and Sañjīva - His chief disciples4
Bhiyyosa and Uttara - His chief disciples4
Tissa and Bhāradvāja - His chief disciples4
King Śuddhodana and Queen Māyā - His parents36
Sāriputta and Moggallāna - His chief disciples46

It's important to note that the information about the earlier Buddhas is primarily found in Buddhist legendary accounts and may vary across different traditions. The most detailed and historically supported information is available for Gautama Buddha36.
